codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly rejects memories with cosine distance < 0.15 to existing memories in the same repository. The cosine_distance helper, find_similar_memory method, and store method modification are all correctly implemented. 8 test cases cover the core functionality including rejection, acceptance, no-embedding bypass, repository isolation, and edge cases.

issues.description: expectedBehavior contains a documentation error: 'Reject if cosine distance < 0.85 (similarity > 0.15)' appears to have wrong threshold values. The feature description correctly states 0.15, and the implementation uses 0.15. The expectedBehavior should likely be 'Reject if cosine distance < 0.15 (similarity > 0.85)' not 'distance < 0.85'. This is a documentation issue in features.json, not an implementation bug.

test_similarity_check_is_repository_scoped only verifies that different-repository similar entries are NOT rejected. It does not verify that same-repository similar entries ARE rejected. The other tests cover rejection, but this test name could be misleading. Consider adding a comment clarifying what is and isn't being tested, or adding an assertion that would fail if repository isolation were broken.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention gaps found. Worker followed AGENTS.md patterns: async_trait usage, error handling with Result<SwellError>, using tracing for logging was not needed for this task.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker used #[async_trait] for trait impl, proper error propagation with ?, and added error variant to swell-core/src/error.rs following existing patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill procedure correctly. No deviations noted in skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback shows followedProcedure: true with no deviations. Worker explored codebase first (grep for MemoryStore trait, MemoryEntry struct, SwellError), then implemented the feature, then ran verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands were used. All verification was done via standard cargo commands documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification used cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-memory -- similarity, cargo fmt -- --check, cargo clippy -p swell-memory -- -D warnings - all documented in .factory/services.yaml

sharedStateObservations.observation: No knowledge gaps identified. Worker correctly understood the MemoryStore trait, MemoryEntry structure, and SwellError enum from the existing codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker grepped for 'trait MemoryStore', 'struct MemoryEntry', and 'enum SwellError' before implementing.

summary: Implementation correctly implements similarity checking for memory deduplication. The feature adds cosine_distance computation, find_similar_memory repository-scoped lookup, and store method modification to reject entries within cosine distance 0.15. All 8 test cases pass. The only issue is a documentation inconsistency in expectedBehavior (0.85 vs 0.15) which does not affect correctness.
